Hero.title: I Become More With You
Hero.subtitle: Jag blir mer med dig
Hero.description: Two lifelong friends fight an anti-racist battle in Sweden, building community through art as a boundless force for change—inside and out.

About.title: About the Film
About.synopsis: Childhood friends MyNa and Farah form the artistic duo Mahoyo. Through their art, they explore questions of identity and belonging within the highly segregated Swedish society. As we follow their journey, we get closer into the growth of far-right movements in Sweden, but also into the uprising of a generation of young Swedes creating political change.
About.fullSynopsis: Childhood friends MyNa and Farah form the artistic duo Mahoyo, acreative platform where they work as DJs, stylists, costume creative platform where they work as DJs, stylists, costume designers, photographers, and filmmakers. MyNa was born in Sweden to a Vietnamese-Chinese family, while Farah was born in Somalia, spent part of her childhood in Kenya, and arrived in Sweden as a child refugee. MyNa is queer, and Farah is Muslim. Growing up together in Oskarshamn—a small town where their identities often made them stand out—their friendship became a refuge, a place of belonging in an environment that could feel isolating. As nearly the only non-white kids in their school, they bonded over their rich and intersecting cultural backgrounds. That connection became the foundation of Mahoyo, through which they explore identity, resistance, and the complexities of belonging in Sweden’s highly segregated society. In this film, we follow MyNa and Farah’s journey as they connect with artists in Vietnam and Kenya, tracing creative lineages that shape their work. Set against the backdrop of Sweden’s growing far-right movements, We witness the rise of a new generation of young Swedes using art and activism to create change.

Mahoyo.label: Directors
Mahoyo.title: Meet
Mahoyo.description:
MyNa Do (they/them) and Farah Yusuf (she/her) are the artistic duo behind Mahoyo — a creative and political practice where art, organising, and community building flow together.

Together with Moira Ganley, they produced and directed The Mahoyo Project: South Africa (2015), which won a SIMA Award, followed by the fiction film Fuck Your Fusion (2020). I Become More With You is their first feature film. Alongside their shared work, MyNa has co-founded Congee and organised queer and BIPOC-centred clubs and events, while Farah most recently directed Holding Light (2023), an artfilm centering the Black experience in Sweden. Both continue this work beyond Mahoyo — through independent projects, organising, and filmmaking.

ScreeningInfo.label: Screen the Film
ScreeningInfo.title: Bring the Film to Your Audience
ScreeningInfo.description: This film is made to be shared. Whether you´re a teacher, youth worker, community organiser, or cultural institution — we want the film to reach the people who need it most. Screenings can be adapted for many different contexts.

StudyMaterial.label: Study Material
StudyMaterial.title: Study Material
StudyMaterial.description: Free downloadable resources for teachers, study circle leaders, and youth workers to use in connection with the film. Suitable for secondary school and adult education contexts.
StudyMaterial.materialADescription: A comprehensive guide with background on the film's themes — identity, belonging, racism, and art as resistance.
StudyMaterial.materialBDescription: Ready-to-use questions for classroom and study circle conversations, organised by theme and age group.
StudyMaterial.materialCDescription: Practical workshop materials exploring how art can be a tool for social change.
StudyMaterial.materialDDescription: Curated books, articles, podcasts and films that connect to the documentary's themes.

ProductionFacts.label: Production Facts
ProductionFacts.cinematographyValue: Kalle Jansson, MyNa Do & Farah Yusuf

Footer.subtitle: Jag blir mer med dig
Footer.description: A documentary about art, identity, and resistance in Sweden.
Footer.contactDescription: For press inquiries, screening requests, and all other questions, please use the form or reach out to our producer Mónica Hernández Rejón.
